crime.value: Murder of Katie at a mill, method unknown, question: how does the tortoise connect to the crime scene?
crime.quote: we have just found our first clue or piece of evidence to little Katie's death

victim.value: Katie, a child, reason for targeting unknown

closed_world.value: Mill and farmhouse property in West Virginia during winter
closed_world.quote: The winter in West Virginia was still getting colder. The tortoise crawled by the mill back to its home

investigator.value: First-person narrator, appears to be official investigator with partners Jim and Garry
investigator.quote: I pick him up. I call Jim and Garry over

suspect_architecture.value: Parents with suspicious reaction to child's death, plus unknown tortoise owner - limited suspect pool with concealed motives
suspect_architecture.quote: when they heard of her death they cried for about a minute and then asked for the body to be buried

red_herring.value: Tortoise as evidence carrier - blood on shell suggests direct involvement but tortoise doesn't belong to farm
red_herring.quote: No tortoises lived there yet we found one, how peculiar.

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence presented to reader simultaneously with investigator - bloody tortoise shell and mill blood smear
clue_fairness.quote: the smudge of blood on his shell went mostly off leaving just a nick from a weapon with someone's blood

social_world.value: Rural farming family structure with mill operation - agricultural hierarchy and family dynamics
social_world.quote: The tortoise crawled by the mill back to its home in the farmhouse

reveal_mechanic.value: Discovery of tortoise eggs in mill suggests breeding location reveals true crime scene
reveal_mechanic.quote: As I took a closer look I noticed the baby tortoise eggs

investigator_wound.value: Investigator trapped alone in mill at night, confronting danger - isolation and vulnerability theme
investigator_wound.quote: Unfortunately I ended up getting locked in the mill. The machines started to come to life

_meta.title: ebook c kelemen the winter mill murder
